title: Melissitus ovalis
uses: Melissitus ovalis, also known as the Oval-leaved Honey Myrtle, is a plant native to Australia with a range of medicinal uses. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used by indigenous Australians to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions. The plant's antimicrobial properties make it effective against bacterial and fungal infections, and it is also used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In addition, the plant's leaves have been used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.

The bark is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves are used as a treatment for fever, inflammation, and as a diuretic.
